question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(e -> e.getType() == EntityType.TOPIC) check in topicExists() which only validates entity type but never validates the entity's memo, key, or ownership fields, allowing subscription to any topic regardless of its configured access controls?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS client certificate validation or gRPC metadata-based authentication in the subscribeTopic() endpoint to subscribe anonymously to any topic, since the service accepts connections without verifying the caller's identity against any topic ownership record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to Long.MAX_VALUE in TopicMessageFilter (which passes @Min(0) validation) to request an astronomically large number of messages from any topic without any ownership check, causing the service to stream the entire topic history and future messages indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Flux pipeline in subscribeTopic() to receive messages from a topic that has been transferred to a new owner, since the topicExists() check is performed only once at subscription time and never re-validates ownership during the lifetime of the subscription?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user receive messages from incomingMessages() that were submitted by a signer using a threshold key, since the function only rebuilds the filter with updated startTime and limit values and never checks whether the subscriber is one of the threshold key signers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnSubscribe(s -> subscriberCount.incrementAndGet()) callback in subscribeTopic() to confirm that their unauthorized subscription was successfully established, using the subscriberCount metric as a side-channel to verify active subscription state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter uses @Builder(toBuilder = true) to create modified filter copies in incomingMessages() and missingMessages() without any re-authorization, meaning a single unauthorized subscription can spawn multiple unauthorized sub-queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic that is part of a regulated financial messaging system requiring signer approval for each reader, since subscribeTopic() has no mechanism to enforce reader-level authorization and delivers messages to any caller who provides a valid topicId?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missingMessages() function's numMissingMessages calculation (current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1) to trigger a large historical query by manipulating the sequence number gap, causing the service to retrieve and deliver a large batch of unauthorized historical messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() thread pool used in the safetyCheck Flux to cause thread pool exhaustion by opening many concurrent subscriptions, each triggering the 1-second safety check, degrading service availability for legitimate authorized subscribers?
